item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This scp hit home, in that I have some understanding of what they’re going through. I have sensory issues that make various negative sensations(especially auditory ones) very hard to ignore as my mind uncontrollably focuses on them. It Turns something that, to everyone else, is barely noticeable into something that is maddening. The idea of having that amplified tenfold sounds like a fate far worse than death

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I remember reading 3404 a while back and really looking forward to more Operation HEALTH stuff. Glad to see you kept this crazy train running. Hearty +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've long thought olfactory, gustatory, tactile, and proprioceptive memes are a big untapped potential, so I definitely approve of this article.

item.title: sadness
item.content:encoded:
First i do want to say that I like this SCP and it's an interesting concept.

But damn I had my eye on this spot ever since I saw that no 4000 contest entry took it. I had my SCP ready to go and was about to take it, but ya beat me to it. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's alright, it's not really crucial to understanding or enjoying the piece. It's more of an easter egg than anything else.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This made me think of a mad, bad dado - in a good way, I hasten to add. As someone with a history of uncomfortable but non-threatening skip conditions it really hit home. Did not catch the tie-in to purity obsessed white supremacy until I read your comment.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Huh. I personally read this as being more of an anomalous version of the Opioid Crisis, since it causes people to lose their ability to cope with even minor pain and drives them to extreme actions and dysfunction as a result.

+1 nonetheless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

You're right in that I was trying to make the victim come off as entitled and overdramatic — see, to draw out the horror in this, I'm trying to tie it thematically to purity-obsessed white supremacy. Obviously that's not in focus here, but an obsession with rights, pure water, perfect white bodies, a birthright… an entitled brat whining about minor inconvenience plays right into that, no?

That said, I didn't want to lose focus of the horror elements of the article, so I made some changes to emphasize that the character feels compelled to avoid pain, rather than just indignant about the fact that he has to.

Glad you enjoyed the article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ show block
– hide block

The interview feels a little weak to me; the subject comes off as whiny and entitled. I realize that's probably the point, but I think the implication this creates is that the anomaly just makes you so pompous and dumb that you engage in self-mutilation to prove a point. It might be worth it to think about pulling back on the interview a smidge and implying that the anomaly causes an obsessive aversion to discomfort, rather than a principled aversion; people do this because they feel like they have to, without necessarily being able to justify this behavior.

That might be counter to your aim here, though.

Regardless: the idea of an anomaly that conflates a failure to experience pain with wellness is interesting, and the sheer self-destructive behavior it promotes is fun and creep-ifying.

item.title: BOLDPOST HAS ARRIVED
item.content:encoded:
I always wanted to build on Operation HEALTH's activities, and I don't think the well of wellness-related horror is dried up, so I decided to make this happen. Next up, something with heart surgery! And probably solvents!

The bit from the end of the monologue is taken from the flavor text of Heidar, Rimewind Master, which has stuck with me for the better part of a decade and that I've only now found the right opportunity to pay tribute.
